title: Bridge Essay: Dissociation and Alterations in Brain Function and Structure: Implications for B

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ultimate/Emptiness** (Phase 17) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Ultimate/Emptiness to mechanistic neuroscience through Dissociation, and, Alterations, in, Brain.

**Dissociation and Alterations in Brain Function and Structure: Implications for Borderline Personality Disorder.** — Curr Psychiatry Rep. PMID: 28138924. DOI: 10.1007/s11920-017-0757-y. Dissociation involves disruptions of usually integrated functions of consciousness, perception, memory, identity, and affect (e.g., depersonalization, derealization, numbing, amnesia, and analgesia). While the precise neurobiological underpinnings of dissociation remain elusive, neuroimaging studies in disorders, characterized by high dissociation (e.g., depersonalization/derealization disorder (DDD), dissociative identity disorder (DID), dissociative subtype of posttraumatic stress disorder (D-
